---
title: "Markup"
---

<div style="height: 2em;"></div>


## Types of markup

**Markup** is plain text with simple symbols that describe how the text should be structured or displayed—for example, as a heading, list, link, or table. Software can read those symbols and turn the text into a formatted document.

+----------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Type     | Example                                                                 |
+==========+=========================================================================+
| Markdown | A `README.md` file or a Quarto document                                 |
+----------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| HTML     | A web page viewed in a browser                                          |
+----------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| LaTeX    | A typeset academic paper; especially great for mathematical expressions |
+----------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| XML      | This is what underlies `.docx`, `.xlsx`, `.pptx`                        |
+----------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------+

## Markdown

Markdown is especially common because it is simple and lightweight. It is used by GitHub, Quarto, Jupyter notebooks, and many other tools. A Markdown file is simply a text file, so it is easy to edit, share, and track with Git.

Common Markdown syntax includes [@xie2026markdown]:

+-----------------+------------------------------------------------------------+
| What you want   | Markdown syntax                                            |
+=================+============================================================+
| Heading         | `# Main heading`, `## Subheading`                          |
+-----------------+------------------------------------------------------------+
| Bold or italics | `**bold**`, `*italics*`                                    |
+-----------------+------------------------------------------------------------+
| Bulleted list   | `- Item`                                                   |
+-----------------+------------------------------------------------------------+
| Numbered list   | `1. Item`                                                  |
+-----------------+------------------------------------------------------------+
| Link            | `[Text](https://example.com)`                              |
+-----------------+------------------------------------------------------------+
| Image           | `![Caption](image.png)`                                    |
+-----------------+------------------------------------------------------------+
| Inline code     | `` `code` ``                                               |
+-----------------+------------------------------------------------------------+
| Code block      | Three backticks on the lines before and after the code     |
+-----------------+------------------------------------------------------------+


::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
## References
:::
